description:
## Problem

Currently, BUCK files for Rust dependencies are generated globally, which may prevent caching opportunities. When any dependency changes, the entire generation process runs again.

## Investigation Areas

### Option 1: Hash-based caching
Cache the BUCK generation by computing a hash from:
- Rust toolchain version/hash
- The dependency itself (crate + version)
- Unified features computation for that dependency

This would allow skipping regeneration for unchanged dependencies.

### Option 2: Per-dependency Nix packages (preferred)
Create a 2nd layer of Nix packages at per-dependency granularity:
- Each dependency gets its own Nix derivation
- The unified set of features is an additional input to the derivation
- Nix's built-in caching handles invalidation automatically
- Cleaner architecture - leverages Nix's strengths

## Benefits
- Faster incremental builds when only some deps change
- Better cache hit rates in CI
- Reduced redundant work

## Questions to Answer
1. How much time is currently spent on global BUCK generation?
2. What's the feature unification algorithm and can it be made per-dep?
3. How would per-dep packages compose in the final cell?

comments.text:
## Investigation Findings (2026-01-21)

### Current Architecture

Cargo.lock -> rustdeps-gen -> rust-deps.toml -> rust-deps-cell.nix -> rustdeps/ cell

The rust-deps-cell.nix orchestrates:
- fetch all crates
- compute-unified-features (global)
- gen-rust-buck (per crate)

**Key insight**: The entire rustdeps cell is a single Nix derivation. When rust-deps.toml changes (even one crate), everything regenerates.

### Key Files Identified

- src/cmd/rustdeps-gen/src/main.rs - Generates rust-deps.toml from Cargo.lock
- src/cmd/compute-unified-features/__main__.py - Global feature unification
- src/cmd/gen-rust-buck/__main__.py - Per-crate BUCK file generation
- nix/buck2/rust-deps-cell.nix - Orchestrates entire cell build
- nix/lib/deps-cell/adapters/rust.nix - Per-crate package builder
- src/python/cargo/features.py - Feature unification algorithm

### Challenge: Feature Unification is Global

The feature unification algorithm needs to know what features ALL crates in the graph request:
1. collect_feature_requirements() - scans all crates for requested features
2. collect_forwarded_features() - handles forwarding like alloc = ["dep/alloc"]
3. expand_features() - expands feature definitions recursively

This is inherently global - crate A's features depend on what crate B requests.

### Proposed Approach (for future work)

Per-crate derivations with shared feature computation:
1. rust-deps.toml -> unified-features.json (single derivation)
2. Each crate gets its own derivation using features.json
3. Compose all crate derivations into final cell

### Next Steps When Resuming

1. Benchmark current generation time
2. Identify if bottleneck is fetching, feature computation, or BUCK generation
3. Prototype per-crate derivations
4. Validate features still unify correctly
comments.author: Yann Hodique

title: Investigate per-dependency BUCK generation for Rust
